story.iWant: to implement semantic response caching với high similarity threshold
story.soThat: I can reduce API calls by 20-40% for semantically similar queries while maintaining response quality

subtasks.subtask: Implement `SemanticCache` class với vector similarity search
Implement cache key generation với semantic hash
Test SemanticCache class với sample queries
Set default similarity threshold to 0.95 (95% similarity)
Make threshold configurable via environment variable
Document threshold configuration và best practices
Implement quality validation checks (human evaluation on samples)
Test quality validation với sample requests
Add auto-disable logic nếu quality drops below threshold
Fallback to exact match caching nếu semantic caching disabled
Test auto-disable mechanism với degraded quality scenarios
Log cache metrics (hits, misses, false positives)
Add cache metrics to monitoring dashboard
Test metrics tracking với sample requests
Compare semantic cached vs non-cached responses

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Semantic similarity threshold được configured (default 0.95, configurable)
Quality validation được integrated để monitor impact
System có thể auto-disable semantic caching nếu quality drops below threshold
Cache hit rate và false positive rate được monitored

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Epic Breakdown: LLM Optimization
doc.snippet: Story 2.1 defines requirements for implementing semantic response caching. Expected savings: $8-15/month with minimal quality impact (<5%). Effort: 4 hours. Prerequisites: Story 1.2, Story 1.4.

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.section: Phase 2: Quality-Preserving Medium Optimizations - Task 2.1: Semantic Response Caching
doc.snippet: Semantic caching requires vector similarity search. High similarity threshold (0.95) to ensure quality. Quality validation and monitoring required. Auto-disable mechanism if quality degrades. Cache metrics tracking (hit rate, false positive rate).

doc.title: Research Prompt: System Prompt Optimization
doc.section: 5. Prompt Caching Implementation - 5.4 Semantic Response Caching
doc.snippet: Semantic response caching uses vector embeddings to find semantically similar queries. Similarity threshold: 0.85-0.95 recommended. Quality validation required. False positive rate should be monitored. Cost reduction: 20-40% for similar queries.

doc.title: Semantic Caching Implementation Guide
doc.snippet: Reference implementation for semantic caching using Redis vector search and sentence embeddings. Shows SemanticCacheManager class structure, vector search implementation, and cache management patterns.

file.reason: New file to create. Implement SemanticCache class với vector similarity search, Redis integration, and quality monitoring.
Primary method to modify for integrating semantic caching. Need to check semantic cache before making LLM calls.
Redis connection initialization. Semantic cache will use existing Redis infrastructure for vector storage.
Configuration management. Can add semantic cache configuration (similarity threshold, quality thresholds) here.

constraint.description: Minimal quality impact (<5%) acceptable. Quality maintained at 95-100% (monitored). High similarity threshold (0.95) required to ensure quality.
Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with semantic caching). Feature flags allow switching and rollback. Quality monitoring required.

Semantic caching requires vector similarity search. Can use sentence-transformers for embeddings, Redis for vector storage, or dedicated vector database (FAISS, ChromaDB). Similarity threshold: Default 0.95 (configurable).

Quality validation required (human evaluation on samples). Quality threshold: 95% similarity required. Auto-disable mechanism if quality drops below threshold. Fallback to exact match caching if semantic caching disabled.
Cache hit rate (semantic matches) and false positive rate (incorrect matches) must be tracked and logged. Metrics should be added to monitoring dashboard.
Must not break existing LLM calls. Semantic caching should be transparent to callers. Fallback to exact match caching if semantic caching fails. Test with existing agent configurations.

interface.name: SemanticCache class
interface.signature: class SemanticCache: def __init__(self, redis_client, similarity_threshold: float = 0.95): ... async def get_cached_response(self, query: str) -> Optional[Dict]: ... async def cache_response(self, query: str, response: Dict[str, Any]): ...

interface.name: make_llm_api_call()
interface.signature: async def make_llm_api_call(messages: List[Dict[str, Any]], model_name: str, ...) -> Union[Dict[str, Any], AsyncGenerator, ModelResponse]

tests.standards: Testing standards include: Quality validation (95-100% similarity check for semantic matches), Performance testing (cache hit rate, false positive rate, cost savings, latency), Integration testing (existing LLM calls, different models, agent configurations, auto-disable mechanism, fallback to exact match caching). Use semantic similarity checking for quality validation. A/B testing framework for comparing original vs optimized modes.

test.idea: Unit test: Verify SemanticCache class creation và initialization
Integration test: Test semantic cache với Redis và vector similarity search
Unit test: Verify similarity threshold configuration (default 0.95, configurable via env var)
Integration test: Test với different threshold values (0.90, 0.95, 0.98) và verify behavior
Unit test: Verify quality validation logic (response_similarity, error_rate tracking)
Integration test: Test quality monitoring với sample requests
Unit test: Verify auto-disable logic (disable semantic caching if quality drops below threshold)
Integration test: Test fallback to exact match caching if semantic caching disabled
Unit test: Verify cache metrics tracking (cache_hit_rate, false_positive_rate)
Integration test: Verify cache metrics are logged và appear in monitoring dashboard
Automated test: Compare semantic cached vs non-cached responses (target: 95-100% similarity)
A/B test: Run same queries with original and optimized modes, compare quality metrics
